In devela::num::grain::niche

devela::num::grain::niche

Struct MaybeNiche 

Source 
#[repr(transparent)]
pub struct MaybeNiche<T: Copy>(pub T);
Expand description

🤷 ⚗️ A zero-cost wrapper that abstracts over niche and non-niche types.

📍 num/grain/niche

MaybeNiche<T> is a transparent wrapper that preserves the representation semantics of T without imposing a niche choice, and introduces no invariants of its own.

It enables niche-agnostic generic code over integer-like representations.

See also NonNiche, which provides an explicit non-optimized representation with the same public API as niche-constrained types.

§Implementations

Implemented for:

  • Primitive integers: u8, u16, u32, u64, u128, usize, i8, i16, i32, i64, i128, isize.
  • NonNiche<T> for all supported primitives.
  • NonZero<T> for all supported primitives.
  • NonValue*<V> for all supported primitives.

§Methods and constants

The API is identical across all implementations (links below point to the u8 specialization).

  • Niche properties: IS_NICHE, is_niche.
  • Contiguity: IS_CONTIGUOUS, is_contiguous.
  • Signedness: HAS_NEGATIVE, has_negative.
  • Bounds: MIN, MAX.
  • Zero: ZERO, has_zero.
  • Construction:
    • From T: new / MaybeNiche(T).
    • From primitive: try_from_prim, from_prim_lossy.
    • From usize: try_from_usize, from_usize_saturating, from_usize_wrapping.
  • Extraction:
    • To T: get/repr.
    • To primitive: get_prim/prim.
    • To usize: try_to_usize, to_usize_saturating, to_usize_wrapping.

Tuple Fields§

§0: T

Implementations§

Source§

impl MaybeNiche<u8>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = false

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: u8) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: u8) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: u8) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: u8) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> u8

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> u8

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u8

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u8

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: u64) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: u64) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: u64) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: u64) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> u64

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> u64

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: i64) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> i64

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> i64

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).

  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.

  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonNiche<u64>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonNiche<u64>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl MaybeNiche<NonNiche<u128>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = false

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonNiche<u128>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: u128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: u128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: u128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonNiche<i64>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonNiche<i64>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl MaybeNiche<NonNiche<i128>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = false

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonNiche<i128>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: i128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: i128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: i128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonZero<u64>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonZero<u64>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl MaybeNiche<NonZero<u128>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = true

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonZero<u128>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: u128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: u128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: u128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonZero<i64>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonZero<i64>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl MaybeNiche<NonZero<i128>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = true

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonZero<i128>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: i128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: i128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: i128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonZero<i128>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonZero<i128>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i128

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i128

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonValueU64<V>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonValueU64<V>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl<const V: u128> MaybeNiche<NonValueU128<V>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = true

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonValueU128<V>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: u128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: u128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: u128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonValueU128<V>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonValueU128<V>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> u128

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> u128

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonValueI64<V>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonValueI64<V>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i64

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i64

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl<const V: i128> MaybeNiche<NonValueI128<V>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = true

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonValueI128<V>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: i128) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: i128) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: i128) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const fn has_negative(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const fn has_zero(self) -> bool

Returns true if this type can represent zero.

Source

pub const fn eq(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self == other.

Source

pub const fn ne(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self != other.

Source

pub const fn lt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self < other.

Source

pub const fn le(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self <= other.

Source

pub const fn gt(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self > other.

Source

pub const fn ge(self, other: Self) -> bool

Returns true if self >= other.

Source

pub const fn get(self) -> NonValueI128<V>

Returns the validated (niche-aware) representation.

Source

pub const fn repr(self) -> NonValueI128<V>

Alias of get, emphasizing representational access.

Source

pub const fn get_prim(self) -> i128

Returns the primitive carrier value.

Source

pub const fn prim(self) -> i128

Alias of get_prim, emphasizing primitive access.

Source

pub const fn try_to_usize(self) -> Result<usize, Overflow> ⓘ

Converts the value into a usize, returning an error on overflow.

§Errors

Will return Overflow if self can’t fit in a usize.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_saturating(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, saturating at the numeric bounds.

Source

pub const fn to_usize_wrapping(self) -> usize

Converts the value into a usize, wrapping at the numeric bounds.

Source§

impl<const V: isize> MaybeNiche<NonValueIsize<V>>

Source

pub const IS_NICHE: bool = true

Whether this representation uses a memory niche for layout optimization.

Source

pub const IS_CONTIGUOUS: bool

Whether the representable domain forms a contiguous interval.

That is, whether every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

Source

pub const HAS_NEGATIVE: bool

Whether the representable domain includes negative values.

Source

pub const MIN: Self

The minimum representable value.

Source

pub const MAX: Self

The maximum representable value.

Source

pub const ZERO: Option<Self>

The zero value, if representable by this type.

Source

pub const fn new(value: NonValueIsize<V>) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche containing value.

Source

pub const fn try_from_prim(primitive: isize) -> Result<Self, InvalidValue> ⓘ

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value.

§Errors
  • InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const unsafe fn from_prim_unchecked(primitive: isize) -> Self

Available on crate feature unsafe_niche only.

Creates a new MaybeNiche without any checks.

§Safety

For niche-optimized types, callers must ensure that value satisfies the variant’s validity constraints.

Source

pub const fn from_prim_lossy(value: isize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a primitive value, converting invalid inputs into a valid but approximate representation.

This constructor performs a lossy conversion, applying a best-effort fallback when the primitive violates the underlying type’s invariant:

  • For NonZero* types, 0 becomes the smallest valid value (MIN).
  • For NonValue* types, conversion defers to their own new_lossy-style semantics.
  • For NonNiche and primitive integers, the value is used as-is.
Source

pub const fn try_from_usize(value: usize) -> Result<Self, NicheValueError> ⓘ

Tries to create a new MaybeNiche from a usize.

§Errors
  • NicheValueError::Overflow if the value cannot be represented by the underlying primitive type.
  • NicheValueError::InvalidValue if the value violates the validity invariant of T.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_saturating(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, saturating at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is saturated to the bounds of the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn from_usize_wrapping(value: usize) -> Self

Creates a new MaybeNiche from a usize, wrapping at numeric bounds.

The conversion applies the following steps:

  1. The usize value is wrapped to the primitive type.
  2. If the resulting value violates the niche invariant of T, a best-effort lossy conversion is applied.
Source

pub const fn is_niche(self) -> bool

Returns true if this representation uses a memory niche.

Source

pub const fn is_contiguous(self) -> bool

Returns true if the representable domain is contiguous.

That is, if every primitive value v such that MIN <= v <= MAX is representable.

impl<T: PartialEq + Copy> PartialEq for MaybeNiche<T>

Source§

fn eq(&self, other: &MaybeNiche<T>) -> bool

Tests for self and other values to be equal, and is used by ==.
1.0.0 (const: unstable) · Source§

fn ne(&self, other: &Rhs) -> bool

Tests for !=. The default implementation is almost always sufficient, and should not be overridden without very good reason.
Source§

impl<T: PartialOrd + Copy> PartialOrd for MaybeNiche<T>

Source§

fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &MaybeNiche<T>) -> Option<Ordering> ⓘ

This method returns an ordering between self and other values if one exists. Read more
1.0.0 (const: unstable) · Source§

fn lt(&self, other: &Rhs) -> bool

Tests less than (for self and other) and is used by the < operator. Read more
1.0.0 (const: unstable) · Source§

fn le(&self, other: &Rhs) -> bool

Tests less than or equal to (for self and other) and is used by the <= operator. Read more
1.0.0 (const: unstable) · Source§

fn gt(&self, other: &Rhs) -> bool

Tests greater than (for self and other) and is used by the > operator. Read more
1.0.0 (const: unstable) · Source§

fn ge(&self, other: &Rhs) -> bool

Tests greater than or equal to (for self and other) and is used by the >= operator. Read more
Source§

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout and non-crate feature safe_code only.

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout and non-crate feature safe_code only.

Performs copy-assignment from self to dest. Read more

where Q: Eq + ?Sized, K: Borrow<Q> + ?Sized,

Checks if this value is equivalent to the given key. Read more

impl<T> From<T> for T

Source§

fn from(t: T) -> T

Returns the argument unchanged.

Source§

impl<T> Hook for T

Source§

fn hook<F>(self, f: F) -> Self
where F: FnOnce(&mut Self),

Hooks a mutation step into the value and returns it. Read more
Source§

fn tap<F>(self, f: F) -> Self
where F: FnOnce(&Self),

Taps into the value for observation and returns it unchanged. Read more
Source§

impl<T, U> Into<U> for T
where U: From<T>,

Source§

fn into(self) -> U

Calls U::from(self).

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of From<T> for U chooses to do.

Source§

impl<T> MemExt for T
where T: ?Sized,

Source§

const NEEDS_DROP: bool = _

Know whether dropping values of this type matters, in compile-time.
Source§

fn mem_align_of<T>() -> usize

Returns the minimum alignment of the type in bytes. Read more
Source§

fn mem_align_of_val(&self) -> usize

Returns the alignment of the pointed-to value in bytes. Read more
Source§

fn mem_size_of<T>() -> usize

Returns the size of a type in bytes. Read more
Source§

fn mem_size_of_val(&self) -> usize

Returns the size of the pointed-to value in bytes. Read more
Source§

fn mem_copy(&self) -> Self
where Self: Copy,

Bitwise-copies a value. Read more
Source§

fn mem_needs_drop(&self) -> bool

Returns true if dropping values of this type matters. Read more
Source§

fn mem_drop(self)
where Self: Sized,

Drops self by running its destructor. Read more
Source§

fn mem_forget(self)
where Self: Sized,

Forgets about self without running its destructor. Read more
Source§

fn mem_replace(&mut self, other: Self) -> Self
where Self: Sized,

Replaces self with other, returning the previous value of self. Read more
Source§

fn mem_take(&mut self) -> Self
where Self: Default,

Replaces self with its default value, returning the previous value of self. Read more
Source§

fn mem_swap(&mut self, other: &mut Self)
where Self: Sized,

Swaps the value of self and other without deinitializing either one. Read more
Source§

unsafe fn mem_zeroed<T>() -> T

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout only.
Returns the value of type T represented by the all-zero byte-pattern. Read more
Source§

unsafe fn mem_transmute_copy<Src, Dst>(src: &Src) -> Dst

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout only.
Returns the value of type T represented by the all-zero byte-pattern. Read more
Source§

fn mem_as_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] ⓘ
where Self: Sync + Unpin,

Available on crate feature unsafe_slice only.
View a Sync + Unpin self as &[u8]. Read more
Source§

fn mem_as_bytes_mut(&mut self) -> &mut [u8] ⓘ
where Self: Sync + Unpin,

Available on crate feature unsafe_slice only.

impl<T, R> Morph<R> for T
where T: ?Sized,

Source§

fn morph<F>(self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(Self) -> R, Self: Sized,

Morphs the value into a new one and returns it. Read more
Source§

fn morph_ref<F>(&self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(&Self) -> R,

Morphs the value by shared reference and returns the result. Read more
Source§

fn morph_mut<F>(&mut self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(&mut Self) -> R,

Morphs the value by exclusive reference and returns the result. Read more
Source§

impl<T> ToOwned for T
where T: Clone,

Source§

type Owned = T

The resulting type after obtaining ownership.
Source§

fn to_owned(&self) -> T

Creates owned data from borrowed data, usually by cloning. Read more
Source§

fn clone_into(&self, target: &mut T)

Uses borrowed data to replace owned data, usually by cloning. Read more
Source§

impl<T, U> TryFrom<U> for T
where U: Into<T>,

Source§

type Error = Infallible

The type returned in the event of a conversion error.
Source§

fn try_from(value: U) -> Result<T, <T as TryFrom<U>>::Error> ⓘ

Performs the conversion.
Source§

impl<T, U> TryInto<U> for T
where U: TryFrom<T>,

Source§

type Error = <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error

The type returned in the event of a conversion error.
Source§

fn try_into(self) -> Result<U, <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error> ⓘ

Performs the conversion.
